creator: jh@FreeBSD.org
text:
On 2009-12-18, Dan Lukes wrote:
>  > if (!nptr && ptr)
>  >     free(ptr);
>  
>  with
>  
>   > if (!nptr && ptr && size > 0)
>   >     free(ptr);
>  
>  Because -
>  
>  When size>0 the previous behavior is maintained.
>  
>  When size==0 and V option not set, then nptr!=NULL (realloc(,0) never 
>  fail) so the ptr is not free - correct behavior.

This is not actually true. When the V flag is not set realloc(ptr, 0)
can fail if ptr == NULL. (It's equivalent to malloc(1).) It is not a
problem however because there's no need to call free() on NULL pointer.

>  When size==0 and V option IS set then we know the ptr is freed by 
>  realloc and it's not freed again here - correct behavior.

-- 
Jaakko
